patches.hypothesis: The issue arises because when custom implementations of mean, variance, or geometric mean are set, they are not properly incremented in the addValue method. The checks should be inverted to ensure that the custom implementations are incremented instead of the default ones.

The refined fix ensures that the implementations are correctly set to instances of Mean, GeometricMean, and Variance before calling increment on them. It adds null checks to prevent NullPointerException and ensures that the objects are of the correct type.
patches.original_hypothesis: The issue arises because when custom implementations of Mean, GeometricMean, and Variance are set, they are not incremented correctly within the addValue method, leading to NaN results.
